title: Weight Loss

jokes.text: This is your last meal. So eat up.
jokes.setup: Michael dramatically announces this is everyone's last meal
jokes.punchline: The absurd over-dramatization for a diet contest
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael treating something mundane as life-or-death dramatic

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This is everyone's last meal.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We're on a diet. Starting tomorrow, no more food. We're all going to starve to death.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's not how diets work, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. We eat salad. Same thing.

jokes.text: From this point forward, you will not use the bathroom. We need to keep our starting weights high so we can lose more.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining contest strategy
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous suggestion to not use the bathroom for weight manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's flawed logic and obsessive competitiveness

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, here's the strategy. We're gonna rig this thing so that I win. First, we cut off Dwight's water supply, so he becomes dehydrated and loses five pounds overnight. Then we convince Ryan that the bathroom is closed, so he holds it in and gains water weight. Classic misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Is it? Or is it genius? I'm thinking genius.

jokes.text: I'm taking the dumplings for my wife. / No, no, no! This is your last meal. There will be no leftovers.
jokes.setup: Kevin wants to take dumplings home
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic enforcement of 'no leftovers' rule
jokes.explanation: Shows both Kevin's food obsession and Michael's control issues

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Can I take these dumplings home?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No. No leftovers. Everything must be consumed here and now.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: But there's like two dozen—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't care if there's a hundred. The rule is the rule. We are a family, and families finish their food together. It's what the Pilgrims would have wanted.

jokes.text: I'm excited to lose weight for the wedding because I really want to have washboard abs the first time Angela sees me naked.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about wedding weight loss goals
jokes.punchline: TMI about wanting abs for Angela to see him naked
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing with misplaced confidence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I've been hitting the gym pretty hard, trying to get these abs in shape before the wedding. Angela's going to see me naked, and I want to look good.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I mean, that's probably too much information, but it's true.

jokes.text: Ex-squeeze me. / No, I will ex-squeeze you.
jokes.setup: Angela says 'ex-squeeze me' as excuse me
jokes.punchline: Andy's literal interpretation and response about squeezing
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Andy's dorky personality

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Ex-squeeze me.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Did you just ask me to squeeze you?

jokes.text: Wait a minute. One more bite of eclair each. Hold it in your mouth if you can't swallow.
jokes.setup: Michael allowing one final bite before weigh-in
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous instruction to hold it in their mouths
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic that perfectly captures Michael's competitive desperation

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, one final bite before we weigh in.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But you have to hold it in your mouth. Don't swallow.

jokes.text: Pam-cake, no, no, no. We would love your extra poundage, but you are going to be leaving us next week, so vamoose.
jokes.setup: Pam steps on the scale
jokes.punchline: Michael's tactless reference to 'extra poundage' while kicking her off
jokes.explanation: Michael's characteristically inappropriate phrasing while trying to be business-focused

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Pam, you're gonna break the scale. Get off of there. Come on, we don't need any extra poundage on that thing.

jokes.text: Pam, you weigh 226 pounds? / Almost, Kevin. / Not almost though, Holly. I mean, not-- not close to 200.
jokes.setup: Kevin misunderstands the total weight reading as Pam's individual weight
jokes.punchline: The awkward clarification and Kevin's continued confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his literal interpretation creates maximum awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Whoa, 300 pounds? Pam, you're not telling us something?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: What? No! That's the total weight of the boxes we're shipping.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh... oh my God, I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: It's okay, Kevin. It happens.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: So... how much do you weigh then?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Kevin!

jokes.text: Math is hard.
jokes.setup: Kevin's mathematical confusion about the weight reading
jokes.punchline: Holly's simple, matter-of-fact observation
jokes.explanation: Holly's dry delivery makes this a perfect capper to Kevin's confusion

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: You weigh 260 pounds. That's what the scale says.

jokes.text: All I had this weekend to eat was a chicken breast and a case of diet coke. / Really? / Yeah. / 'Cause I haven't eaten anything since noon on Friday.
jokes.setup: Kelly claims to have eaten very little
jokes.punchline: Ryan's casual one-upmanship with an even more extreme claim
jokes.explanation: Captures both characters' competitive nature and unhealthy dieting approaches

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I barely ate anything today. I had like half a granola bar.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well I didn't eat anything. I just absorbed nutrients through my skin.

jokes.text: I happen to be losing weight on my own for my own reasons. The truth is I have lost a little of my speed. A little of my fire.
jokes.setup: Stanley talking about personal weight loss motivation
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim that he ever had 'speed' or 'fire'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Stanley's delusion about his past athleticism is perfectly deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Stanley Hudson
dialogue.line: I lost 40 pounds. My doctor said I had to lose weight or I would die. I said, 'Doc, I don't wanna brag, but I think I've been dying for a while now.' So he said, 'You need to exercise.' I said, 'Doc, I don't run. I don't jog. If I wanted to get somewhere fast, I'd take the car.'

jokes.text: Here's what I used to look like. Look at those biceps. We were fighting the power and eating whatever we wanted.
jokes.setup: Stanley showing a photo of his younger self
jokes.punchline: The presumably unimpressive photo he's describing as muscular and rebellious
jokes.explanation: Visual gag combined with Stanley's delusional self-image creates strong comedy

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: This is me back in the day. I was pretty muscular, kind of a rebel.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was a real troublemaker.

jokes.text: Jimbo. / Ah, they moved the shower. / Did you see Holly's butt? / Nope, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Creed casually asking Jim about Holly's butt
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate, firm denial
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed inappropriate comment with Jim as straight man

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: So what's the deal with Holly's butt?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. No. Absolutely not. We're not doing this.

jokes.text: Because most of the time friends don't talk about other friends' butts.
jokes.setup: Creed asking about Holly's butt
jokes.punchline: Jim's reasonable but pointed explanation of social norms
jokes.explanation: Jim's measured response to Creed's inappropriateness

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Why is nobody talking about Holly's butt?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Because that would be inappropriate.

jokes.text: And that her butt refuses to quit!
jokes.setup: Creed listing normal facts he learned about Holly
jokes.punchline: The sudden inappropriate comment after seemingly normal observations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed escalation - builds up normal conversation then drops inappropriate bomb

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Holly's from Mojave. She has two dogs. Her favorite movie is Beaches.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: She's allergic to shellfish.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: And I've never seen her skin.

jokes.text: That's insane. I thought you had to pee. / I'll just go later.
jokes.setup: Jim pointing out Creed's excuse for being in the bathroom area
jokes.punchline: Creed abandoning his bathroom excuse to continue the conversation
jokes.explanation: Shows Creed's priorities and lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Weren't you just in the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm done now.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm a lesbian. / I'm gay. / I'm not a lesbian. I don't know why I said that. Stupid joke.
jokes.setup: Holly trying to deflect Michael's matchmaking
jokes.punchline: Her immediate awkward backtrack and explanation
jokes.explanation: Holly's social awkwardness creates relatable cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I'm not looking to date right now. I'm really focused on my career.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Come on, Holly. You're a catch.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: No, I mean... I'm just... I have a boyfriend. In New York. We're very serious.

jokes.text: Oh, you think it's a choice?
jokes.setup: Holly joking about switching to women
jokes.punchline: Michael's serious response about sexual orientation being a choice
jokes.explanation: Michael's well-meaning but problematic response creates awkward moment

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's not funny. Sexual orientation is not a choice. I have a very good friend who is gay, and he didn't choose that. And even if he did choose it, that would be fine too.

jokes.text: I'm messing with you, Holly. / I knew that. Okay, bye.
jokes.setup: Holly's obvious discomfort with the conversation
jokes.punchline: Her transparent lie about knowing he was joking while fleeing
jokes.explanation: Holly's awkward exit captures the discomfort perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was just joking.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I know you were! I knew that the whole time.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I'm going to go... do something else now.

jokes.text: I will be a little fish in the big apple. What up, 212?
jokes.setup: Pam talking about going to New York
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt at New York slang with the area code
jokes.explanation: Pam trying to be cool but coming off dorky is endearing

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm going to New York! I'm so excited. I'm gonna be all like, 'Yo, what's up, 212!'

jokes.text: It's not gonna take her ten minutes to fax it, Jim.
jokes.setup: Jim saying Pam leaves in ten minutes
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal interpretation of how long faxing takes
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight missing the emotional subtext for literal logistics

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pam leaves in ten minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Ten minutes? That's not nearly enough time to fax something to corporate and get a response back. The fax machine is on the third floor, and with the current paper jam situation, we're looking at a minimum of fifteen to twenty minutes for a round-trip transmission.

jokes.text: Ang... Ela. Ela ela ela under my angerela ela ela ela eh eh eh--
jokes.setup: Andy singing to Angela
jokes.punchline: His mangled version of Rihanna's 'Umbrella' with Angela's name
jokes.explanation: Andy's awkward romantic gesture through mangled pop song is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Ella, ella, eh, eh, eh. Angela, Angela!

jokes.text: Every little boy fantasizes about his fairytale wedding.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about wedding venue planning
jokes.punchline: The gender reversal of typical wedding fantasies
jokes.explanation: Subverts gender expectations while staying true to Andy's character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what's funny about wedding planning? All these years, we're told women dream about their perfect wedding day. But you know what I've been fantasizing about? A venue with a killer sound system, an open bar, and nobody asking me to wear a boutonnière.

jokes.text: No, no. This is where my parents decided not to get divorced.
jokes.setup: Beautiful mansion setting seeming romantic
jokes.punchline: The dark family history twist
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reveals Andy's family dysfunction in unexpected way

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Welcome to my family's ancestral mansion. Isn't it beautiful?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I know what you're thinking - 'Andy, how did your family acquire such a magnificent estate?' Well, let me tell you about the Bernard family history...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It turns out my great-grandfather made his fortune in... let's just say 'questionable' business practices. Murder, embezzlement, a little light treason.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And my grandfather? He was worse. He once said, and I quote, 'The only good neighbor is a dead neighbor.' He meant it literally.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So yeah, this beautiful romantic mansion? Built on a foundation of crime, deceit, and at least three unsolved murders. Now, shall we dance?

jokes.text: Oh, we done good in there, half-pint. / Well, that was the last time, Dwight. I mean it.
jokes.setup: Dwight and Angela clearly just had sex based on context
jokes.punchline: Angela's post-coital regret and Dwight's satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Captures the awkward affair dynamic between these characters perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: This was a mistake. This never should have happened.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A mistake? Angela, that was the most powerful moment of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: We work together, Dwight. This is completely inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Inappropriate? I've been waiting years for this. You felt it too, I know you did.

jokes.text: I wrote you a good-bye poem. It's really long. I left it... Up in my office. Could you just please wait till I go get it?
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic goodbye to Pam
jokes.punchline: His unprepared desperation and the poem he doesn't actually have ready
jokes.explanation: Michael's dramatic but unprepared gesture is characteristically inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Pam, I want you to know that you've meant so much to me. I prepared a poem for this moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Roses are red, violets are blue... I'm making this up as I go, aren't I?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Look, the point is, you're like the daughter I never had. Or the sister. Or the... best friend who I'm weirdly emotional about leaving.

jokes.text: The last word is 'seagulls.' / I'm sure it was really lovely. / I took a lot from other poems.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to describe his poem
jokes.punchline: His admission to plagiarizing other poems
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual admission to plagiarism while trying to be heartfelt

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I wrote this poem myself. Well, actually, I compiled it from various sources.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You mean you plagiarized it?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I prefer to think of it as 'creative sampling.'

jokes.text: Something about a guy who used to work here.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining Pam's views on long engagements
jokes.punchline: The ironic reference to Roy without naming him
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback that rewards long-time viewers without being obvious

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pam thinks long engagements are bad. She says they give people too much time to, uh, run off with someone else's fiancée.

jokes.text: I can't believe I'm saying this, but Michael is actually killing it with Holly. And I think I know why. It's because Holly is kind of a major dork.
jokes.setup: Jim analyzing Michael's success with Holly
jokes.punchline: The realization that Holly's dorkiness matches Michael's
jokes.explanation: Insightful character observation that explains an unlikely pairing

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's like Michael finally met someone as dorky as he is. They're like two puzzle pieces of awkwardness that somehow fit together perfectly.

jokes.text: I'm MC Mike Scott, and I am hot she's DJ Jazzy Flax, and she is the best
jokes.setup: Michael and Holly doing a rap about the weight loss
jokes.punchline: Their dorky DJ names and awkward rap performance
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael and Holly's compatible dorkiness through cringey performance

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yo, yo, yo. I'm MC Soft Hands.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: And I'm DJ Sweet Cheeks.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We're here to talk about healthy eating and exercise.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Lose the weight, don't be a hater.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Word.

jokes.text: I don't really know Ronnie. But I have a feeling I will get to know her very well over the next few years. And eventually declare my love for her.
jokes.setup: Kevin seeing new receptionist Ronnie for the first time
jokes.punchline: His immediate romantic projection and inevitable love declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his romantic delusions with any woman he meets

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Wow. Hi. I'm Kevin. From the warehouse.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Hi Kevin, I'm Ronnie.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Ronnie. That's a beautiful name. You know, I think we're soulmates. I'm going to marry you.

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late. I accidentally switched my alarm clock setting to Zapf Chancery.
jokes.setup: Pam arriving late to art class
jokes.punchline: The impossibly specific and nerdy excuse involving font names
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific excuse that only Pam would think of

jokes.text: You're shaking. Are you all right? / Just leave me alone! I am on the third day of my cleanse diet.
jokes.setup: Professor noticing Kelly's distress
jokes.punchline: Kelly's aggressive response and extreme diet explanation
jokes.explanation: Kelly's extreme diet commitment creating public scenes

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: Kelly, are you okay? You seem upset.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Upset? I'm fine! I'm great! I'm on a new diet where I only eat things that are angry at me first. So far I've had two gas station coffees and a very confrontational apple.

jokes.text: I just bought some bikinis online, size two. So... Gonna look amazing
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining her extreme diet motivation
jokes.punchline: The delusional confidence about fitting into size two bikinis
jokes.explanation: Kelly's unrealistic expectations and materialistic motivation

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I'm on this new diet where I only eat foods that are a different color than when they were alive. So I can eat a carrot because it's orange and it was orange when it was alive. But I can't eat a blueberry because it's blue and it was blue when it was alive.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I'm gonna be a size two by summer. I'm gonna look amazing in a bikini.

jokes.text: You remember last week when that girl went missing? Guess whose candles they used for the vigil? / Cool. / Thank god they found her too. / Oh, they found her?
jokes.setup: Jan bragging about her candles being used for a vigil
jokes.punchline: Her focus on the business opportunity over the human tragedy, plus not knowing the outcome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Jan's complete self-absorption and business obsession

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: They used my candles for a vigil. Do you know how much exposure that is? That's huge for my business.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: A vigil for what?

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I don't know, some tragedy or something. But Jim, think about it — my candles were there.

jokes.text: When Michael told us that Jan was pregnant, he led us to believe that he was the father... By telling us that he was the father.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the pregnancy situation
jokes.punchline: The deadpan clarification of Michael's direct statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect talking head structure with Jim's characteristic dry delivery

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So Michael just came out and said it directly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Pam is pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, with a baby.

jokes.text: Yes, she is. And clinically insane.
jokes.setup: Holly commenting that Jan is beautiful
jokes.punchline: Oscar's matter-of-fact addition about her mental state
jokes.explanation: Oscar's clinical assessment delivered with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Yeah, she's beautiful, but she's also insane.

jokes.text: No! You do not talk to him like that! / But he's an idiot! / He is not an idiot. / Thank you, Holly. / He is mentally challenged. But he's doing a super job here.
jokes.setup: Holly defending Kevin from Angela's insults
jokes.punchline: Holly's well-meaning but problematic assumption about Kevin's mental capacity
jokes.explanation: Holly's misguided defense creates maximum awkwardness for everyone involved

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Kevin is an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: That's not fair, Angela. Kevin is a very smart man. He just has a different way of learning things.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Holly, he thought a podcast was a jacket.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Well, maybe he's just not auditory. Kevin, you're more of a visual learner, right?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I don't know what that means.

jokes.text: Do you think that I'm retarded?
jokes.setup: Holly calling Kevin mentally challenged
jokes.punchline: Kevin's direct, confused question about his mental capacity
jokes.explanation: Kevin's innocent confusion creates peak uncomfortable moment

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Kevin, you're mentally challenged.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Mentally challenged? Or like, like, I'm not smart?

jokes.text: Dangerous, tacky, sharks, haunted-- no.
jokes.setup: Andy listing various wedding venue options
jokes.punchline: Angela's rapid-fire dismissal of each option with single words
jokes.explanation: Angela's economy of words and pickiness perfectly captured

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: No. No. Absolutely not. No. No. No. No. No. No. No.

jokes.text: I have a nice comforter and several cozy pillows. I usually read a chapter of a book, and it's lights out by 8:30. That's how I sleep at night.
jokes.setup: Talking about sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Angela's overly detailed and boring nighttime routine
jokes.explanation: Angela's rigidly boring lifestyle presented as if it's interesting

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, I have a very specific nighttime routine. First, I lay out my clothes for the next day in the order I'll put them on. Then I brush my teeth for exactly two minutes—not a second more, not a second less. After that, I floss each tooth individually, which takes about fifteen minutes. Then I use my tongue scraper, my water pick, and my electric toothbrush again just to be safe. I apply my night cream in upward strokes only, never downward, as that causes sagging. Then I set my alarm for 5 AM, 5:15 AM, 5:30 AM, and 5:45 AM. Then I say my prayers, which is usually about forty-five minutes, and then I lie in bed completely still for exactly eight hours before getting up and starting again.

jokes.text: I'm gonna watch HGTV, and maybe go to Bungalow 8 with John Mayer.
jokes.setup: Pam describing her evening plans
jokes.punchline: The casual drop of clubbing with a celebrity as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Pam's attempt at sounding cosmopolitan with impossible celebrity namedrop

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah, so tonight I'm probably just gonna grab some dinner, maybe catch up on some shows.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Oh, and then I'm going clubbing with Beyoncé.

jokes.text: We call her Rice-a-Ronnie. She is hilarious. She is wonderful. A beam of light in this dark, dark office. / Not really so much.
jokes.setup: Michael's effusive praise of Ronnie
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's immediate deadpan contradiction of his assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of Michael's hyperbolic praise with deadpan reality

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Ronnie, you are an absolute legend. Your work ethic is unmatched, your dedication is inspiring, and frankly, you're one of the best employees I've ever had the privilege of managing.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: That's not true at all. I'm lazy, I'm unreliable, and I hate working here.

jokes.text: The nerve. / Oh, calm down, weirdo. It's just a joke. She's such a weirdo!
jokes.setup: Michael calling Ronnie out for her deadpan response
jokes.punchline: His immediate flip to insulting her then back to praising her weirdness
jokes.explanation: Michael's inconsistent reaction to Ronnie's personality

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Why do you always have to be so dead inside?

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: I'm not dead inside.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You're the worst. No, actually, you know what? That deadpan thing you do is kind of amazing.

jokes.text: I was at the hospital. I lost four pounds. I don't know what those other fools were doing, but I lost four pounds.
jokes.setup: Team only lost one pound collectively
jokes.punchline: Stanley's pride in his individual contribution while dismissing everyone else
jokes.explanation: Stanley's typical self-centered perspective on team efforts

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I lost two pounds.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

jokes.text: I once went 28 years without having sex. And then again for seven years.
jokes.setup: Angela talking about willpower
jokes.punchline: Her casual revelation about extended periods of celibacy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment revealing Angela's sexual history with perfect deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: It's all about willpower. I haven't had sex in three years.

jokes.text: Shotgun weddings. / That's not what that is. / Fright. Being scared to death.
jokes.setup: Question about number one cause of death
jokes.punchline: Kevin's misunderstanding of 'shotgun weddings' as literal death cause
jokes.explanation: Kevin's literal interpretation creates confusion about common phrases

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What do you think is the number one cause of death?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Shotgun weddings.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: You know, shotgun weddings. People get shot at weddings all the time.

jokes.text: Cryogenics. Spare me five.
jokes.setup: Michael asking how they'll all live forever
jokes.punchline: His casual suggestion of cryogenics while asking for five pounds weight loss
jokes.explanation: Michael's absurd solution delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: How are we all gonna live forever?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We could do cryogenics. I'll do it if you guys do it. But I want to lose five pounds first.

jokes.text: I don't want you to end up with a surprise pregnancy like me.
jokes.setup: Jan giving Jim advice about safe sex
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to her own 'surprise' pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Jan's inappropriate oversharing with employees

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: You need to be responsible about these things, Jim. Use protection.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I'm serious. I didn't use protection once, and I got a surprise pregnancy.

jokes.text: All I care about is that we're gonna spend the rest of our lives together.
jokes.setup: Andy's romantic speech to Angela about wedding venues
jokes.punchline: The ironic sincerity given their doomed relationship
jokes.explanation: Andy's genuine sentiment becomes tragic given the audience's knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Angela, I've been thinking about our wedding venues, and I want to make sure we pick the perfect place. Somewhere romantic, somewhere that means something to us. Because what we have is special, and I want our wedding day to reflect that.

jokes.text: Stanley, now all I like is baklava.
jokes.setup: Stanley saying he doesn't like cake anymore
jokes.punchline: His specific and random new food preference
jokes.explanation: Stanley's oddly specific new taste preference fits his character

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I don't like cake anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: Now I'm into pudding skins. Just the skins.

jokes.text: Not that guy who murdered his mother. He was not so handsome. Also Kevin.
jokes.setup: Holly saying Scranton men are handsome
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of a murderer and Kevin in the same breath as exceptions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy putting Kevin on same level as a murderer for unattractiveness

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Scranton men are handsome.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Well, except for the serial killer and Kevin.

jokes.text: I swallowed a tapeworm last night. It's going to grow up to three feet inside of me, and then it eats all my food so that I don't get fat. And then after three months, I take some medicine and then I pass it. Creed sold it to me. It's from Mexico. / That wasn't a tapeworm.
jokes.setup: Creed's extreme weight loss method involving a tapeworm
jokes.punchline: Ryan's casual revelation that it wasn't actually a tapeworm
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed insanity with perfect deadpan revelation at the end

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I've lost 30 pounds in two weeks. My secret? A tapeworm.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, about that... it wasn't actually a tapeworm. It was just a regular worm I found in the parking lot.

jokes.text: I'm keeping a list of everyone who wrongs me, so when I'm back on top, they'll be sorry. Kevin just made the list.
jokes.setup: Ryan's revenge fantasy
jokes.punchline: Adding Kevin to his enemies list for a minor slight
jokes.explanation: Ryan's petty vindictiveness and delusions of future power

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Kevin... you think you're so funny with your chili, but I'm going to remember this. I'm adding you to the list.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Right between Dwight and that barista who got my order wrong in 2008.

jokes.text: I've even started, um, volunteering. Giving back to the community. / That's great. / You're talking about your court-ordered community service? / I don't need a judge to tell me to keep my community clean. / But he did, right?
jokes.setup: Ryan claiming to volunteer
jokes.punchline: Jim's persistent questioning revealing it's court-ordered
jokes.explanation: Jim methodically exposing Ryan's attempt to spin his legal troubles

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I volunteer to help with the charity drive.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's great, Ryan. How many hours can you commit?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Uh, 40 hours.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Interesting. And this is completely voluntary?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yes, totally voluntary.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So you're choosing to do this of your own free will?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Well, my lawyer said it would look good...

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Your lawyer? Ryan, is this court-ordered?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: It's community service, Jim. But I'm volunteering my attitude.

jokes.text: This is the hardest thing I've ever had to do in my entire life. I haven't had a very hard life.
jokes.setup: Kelly describing the difficulty of dieting
jokes.punchline: Her immediate acknowledgment that her life has been easy
jokes.explanation: Kelly's rare moment of self-awareness about her privileged life

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Dieting is really hard. I mean, it's not like I have a lot of willpower to begin with.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: But you know what? My life has been really easy. So I guess that makes sense.

jokes.text: I wanted to say I'm sorry for treating you bad the past couple of years. I-- I was in my mid-twenties, and... I was going through a lot of stuff. I think I never really processed 9/11.
jokes.setup: Ryan apologizing to Kelly
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous excuse of not processing 9/11 for his bad behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment - using national tragedy as excuse for personal failings

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Kelly, I'm sorry. I just... I haven't processed 9/11 yet.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Ryan, that was like ten years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I know, but I'm a very deep person.

jokes.text: Phyllis Vance, ladies and gentlemen! / You left me in a bad part of town. / Yeah, I took your purse. What are you worried about?
jokes.setup: Dwight celebrating Phyllis's weight loss
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he stole her purse when abandoning her
jokes.explanation: Reveals the extent of Dwight's callousness in his scheme

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Phyllis, I have to say, you're looking trim. Did you lose weight?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Thank you, Dwight. I've been working out.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, it shows. You know, I noticed you were getting a bit heavy, so I took the liberty of hiding your purse during our sales calls. You had to walk everywhere. It was actually an intervention.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: You what?

jokes.text: This big fat pig is beautiful.
jokes.setup: Michael in sumo suit talking about beauty
jokes.punchline: His tone-deaf description of himself as a 'big fat pig'
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at body positivity undercut by poor word choice

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm like a big fat pig.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But I'm a beautiful pig.

jokes.text: Fat people are not monsters. / Bang, boom! Case in point.
jokes.setup: Michael defending fat people
jokes.punchline: Holly immediately making monster noises, contradicting his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing undermines Michael's serious point

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Fat people are just big-boned. There's nothing wrong with them.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: RAAAAWR! GRRRRAAAAAHHHHH!

jokes.text: Hell of an ass.
jokes.setup: People listing what they like about Kelly's appearance
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt, inappropriate assessment
jokes.explanation: Kevin's characteristic inappropriate bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Her skin looks like it could use some lotion.

jokes.text: I wonder what people like about me. Probably my jugs.
jokes.setup: People complimenting Kelly's appearance
jokes.punchline: Her casual self-assessment of her own assets
jokes.explanation: Kelly's typical self-objectification delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I'm not just a pretty face. I'm also extremely delusional about my own appearance.

jokes.text: Shaved off my goatee. I am goatee-less. We are the goat-less brothers. / Yeah, yep!
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to bond with Ryan over facial hair
jokes.punchline: The awkward wordplay and Ryan's uncomfortable response
jokes.explanation: Kevin's desperate attempt to connect through weak wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: So Ryan, I've been thinking about growing out my beard. You know, really let it get thick and full.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I figure with my facial hair, I could really make a statement. You know what I'm saying?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I'm really not sure where you're going with this.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, we could be beard brothers. Get it? Brothers?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got it. That's... that's not happening.

jokes.text: I'm gonna write you both up for not working. / I'm gonna write you up for not working. / Okay. Well played. / Neither of us will write the other up for not working.
jokes.setup: Jim and Dwight threatening to write each other up
jokes.punchline: The mutual assured destruction standoff
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim-Dwight dynamic reaching logical conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You write me up, I write you up.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You wouldn't dare.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Try me.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have documentation on everything you've done wrong for the past five years.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And I have photos of you eating tuna fish at your desk.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not even illegal.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It should be.

jokes.text: Pam, will you marry me? / Oh, my god! / So? / Yes!
jokes.setup: Jim's surprise proposal at a rest stop
jokes.punchline: The romantic moment in an utterly unromantic location
jokes.explanation: Subverts romantic proposal expectations with perfect character motivation

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pam, I know this isn't the fanciest place, but I wanted to ask you something.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim, are we really doing this at a rest stop?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Will you marry me?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yes! Oh my God, yes!

jokes.text: I went zip-lining my third day in Costa Rica. I... Guess the harness wasn't strapped in exactly right. I broke my neck. And... I've been in the hospital five weeks now. Still haven't seen the beach.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his absence
jokes.punchline: The casual way he describes a serious injury and his misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Ryan's priorities remain skewed even after major injury

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, so I got hit by a bus.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: But the important thing is, my laptop was fine.

tmdb_overview: Michael tries to motivate the Scranton branch to lose weight as part of an interoffice competition. Jim is lost after Pam leaves for art school in New York, and Angela continues to see Dwight even though she is now engaged to Andy.
insight: Season opener's cringe carries a thin 79—66 jokes land unevenly across the hour.
